name: Vigorish Viper
description: Publicly disclosed by Infoblox in 2024. Operates a sprawling traffic distribution and AiTM infrastructure linked to illegal gambling and credential harvesting. 170K+ associated domains tracked.

killChainTemplate.description: okta-acme-login.live registered via privacy-protected registrar.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Threat Defense intercepts via Infoblox NRD + Viper-cluster signature.

killChainTemplate.description: Targeted phishing to 12 finance staff with credential-harvest links.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks pre-delivery for 11 of 12 endpoints — 1 unprotected.

killChainTemplate.description: Unprotected endpoint follows link; AiTM captures Okta session token.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Endpoint protection drift — Twin flags as exposure.

killChainTemplate.description: Captured session replayed against AD federation endpoint.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Token-replay anomaly — SOC Insights opens incident.

killChainTemplate.description: Lateral movement attempt to financial database via SQLnet.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Contained at hop 2 with deployed controls.

name: VexTrio Viper
description: World's largest DNS-based Traffic Distribution System. Routes victims to scams, banking trojans, and tech-support fraud across 70K+ partner domains. Disclosed by Infoblox in early 2024.

killChainTemplate.description: Malspam delivers TDS link to payroll team.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks via Infoblox VexTrio TDS signature.

killChainTemplate.description: Payload attempted on finance endpoints.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks resolution of tds-route.top across finance pool.

killChainTemplate.description: Stealer attempts to scrape Workday session cookies.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks outbound C2 — no creds reach VexTrio.

killChainTemplate.description: Stealer attempts to exfiltrate Workday session and payroll data.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Agentic Takedown filed registrar abuse report — domain seized in 2h.

name: Savvy Seahorse
description: DNS CNAME-based traffic distribution and AiTM infrastructure that aged domains for months before activation — bypassing newly-observed-domain feeds.

killChainTemplate.description: Long-aged domain matures across legitimate traffic — bypasses NRD feeds.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Domain aged beyond NRD threshold — Twin surfaces as drift.

killChainTemplate.description: Highly-tailored lure pretending to be board calendar.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Reputation feeds clean — needs SOC Insights anomaly model.

killChainTemplate.description: Session token replayed against AD federation endpoint.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin recommends MFA hardening on exec cluster.

killChainTemplate.description: Identity-based discovery against Vault — searching for secrets.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin flags lack of R&D segmentation — segment R&D from corporate AD.

name: Decoy Dog
description: Pupy-RAT-derived remote access toolkit using DNS as its primary C2 channel. Discovered by Infoblox in 2023 — operates at very low DNS-query volumes to evade detection.

killChainTemplate.description: Slow, periodic DNS queries to beacons-cdn.io.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Infoblox long-dwell DNS pattern fires — TD blocks.

killChainTemplate.description: Reused engineering credentials from prior breach data.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks resolution of Decoy Dog C2 from jump host.

killChainTemplate.description: Discovery against Vault — secrets enumeration.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Recommend Vault audit log forwarding to SOC Insights.

name: APT29 (Cozy Bear)
tigSource: CISA, NCSC, Mandiant — corroborated by Infoblox DNS telemetry
description: Russian state-sponsored long-dwell operator behind SolarWinds, MIDNIGHT BLIZZARD, and the 2023 M365 intrusions. Targets identity infrastructure and email. Goes very quiet between bursts.

killChainTemplate.description: Stolen M365 service principal token from a third-party SaaS breach.
killChainTemplate.explanation: No detection at acquisition — Twin flags token usage anomaly post-hoc.

killChainTemplate.description: Privileged consent grant to attacker-owned OAuth app via Entra.
killChainTemplate.explanation: SOC Insights surfaces unusual OAuth consent — held pending human review.

killChainTemplate.description: Adds GlobalAdmin to attacker principal in shadow path.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin recommends conditional-access hardening + privileged role review.

killChainTemplate.description: Mass mailbox pull of exec inbox via Graph API.
killChainTemplate.explanation: SOC Insights anomaly fires on Graph API volume — token revoked.

name: Scattered Spider (UNC3944)
tigSource: Mandiant, CrowdStrike, CISA — corroborated by Infoblox DNS telemetry
description: Financially-motivated group behind the MGM and Caesars intrusions. Specializes in phone-based social engineering against IT helpdesks to reset MFA, then ransomware via ALPHV affiliate. Devastating against orgs with weak helpdesk verification.

killChainTemplate.description: Phone call to IT helpdesk impersonating EXEC-WS-CFO user — requests MFA reset.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin flags as drift — helpdesk verification workflow does not pull Twin identity context.

killChainTemplate.description: MFA factor reset and re-enrollment from attacker device.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Okta MFA policy permits self-reset post-helpdesk — Twin recommends hardening.

killChainTemplate.description: Lateral move via legitimate VPN + RDP using compromised account.
killChainTemplate.explanation: SOC Insights flags impossible-travel; access held for human approval.

killChainTemplate.description: Attempts to clear backup vault catalogs before ransomware fires.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Backup vault immutability policy blocks delete — TD also blocks attacker C2 resolution.

killChainTemplate.description: ALPHV ransomware payload deployment to crown jewel.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks C2 retrieval — payload cannot execute without key.

name: ALPHV / BlackCat
description: Rust-written ransomware-as-a-service. Affiliates use stolen creds + Scattered-Spider-style social engineering. Famous for the Change Healthcare breach. Specializes in deleting backup catalogs before encrypting.

killChainTemplate.description: Stolen VPN creds from initial access broker.
killChainTemplate.explanation: TD blocks resolution of alphv-collect.onion proxy — affiliate cannot establish C2.

killChainTemplate.description: BloodHound-style domain enumeration.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Volume anomaly on LDAP — SOC Insights opens incident.

killChainTemplate.description: Double-extortion: 800 GB exfil to leakdata-svc.top before encryption.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks leak site resolution — exfil aborted.

killChainTemplate.description: Encrypts backup vault to prevent recovery.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Backup immutability policy holds — Rubrik snapshot cannot be deleted.

name: FIN7
tigSource: Mandiant, Mitre ATT&CK · corroborated by Infoblox DNS telemetry
description: One of the most prolific financially-motivated groups. Pioneered Carbanak-style intrusions against banks and retailers. Pivoted to ransomware affiliate work after 2022 indictments. Targets POS and payment infrastructure.

killChainTemplate.description: Weaponized invoice document with malicious macro.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Inflight email security strips attachment pre-delivery.

killChainTemplate.description: One unprotected endpoint opens attachment.
killChainTemplate.explanation: CrowdStrike Falcon prevents execution — payload quarantined.

killChainTemplate.description: Attempts to query SAP financial reporting tables.
killChainTemplate.explanation: Twin recommends data-loss-prevention policy on SAP reports.

killChainTemplate.description: Exfil to sap-reports.live — FIN7 staging server.
killChainTemplate.explanation: DNS Armor blocks resolution — agentic takedown notified.
